name: The Go Engineer v3 — Advanced Electives

architectural_decision.decision: Break the locked v2 curriculum and rebuild around the Report 1 phase-based zero-to-software-engineer path.
architectural_decision.v2_policy: curriculum.v2.json is frozen legacy import data, not the active source of truth.
architectural_decision.core_rule: Keep good lessons, break the old path, optimize for learner progression, proof, documentation quality, and job readiness.
architectural_decision.full_bundle_policy: Split files are authoritative. curriculum.v3.full.generated.json is an optional generated snapshot for review/export only.

modules.title: Advanced Electives
modules.learning_goal: Keep advanced Go and backend topics without blocking the core learner path.
modules.summary: Keep advanced Go and backend topics without blocking the core learner path.

items.title: Generics
items.learning_objective: Understand and apply Generics in the context of professional Go software engineering.
required_prior_knowledge: Complete the core path through Module 15 before starting advanced electives.

zero_magic.problem_solved: This lesson explains what problem Generics solves for a Go developer.
zero_magic.why_it_exists: Generics exists because real software needs this concept to become understandable, reliable, or maintainable.
zero_magic.mental_model: Think of Generics as one step in the learner's path from raw code to professional engineering judgment.
zero_magic.under_the_hood: The lesson must explain the underlying mechanics rather than asking the learner to memorize behavior.
zero_magic.how_go_uses_it: The lesson shows how Go expresses Generics with idiomatic standard-library-first examples.
beginner_mistakes: Using Generics mechanically without understanding the reason.
Skipping error paths or edge cases.
Copying code without being able to explain the behavior.
zero_magic.real_world_usage: Professional Go code uses Generics in production code, tests, tools, services, or operational workflows.
zero_magic.proof_of_understanding: The learner must complete the practice task and explain the result in plain language.

builds_on.label: Module 15.15 — Demo preparation
builds_on.reason: This lesson depends on the target concept.

proof.practice_task: Complete a focused exercise that applies Generics.
proof.expected_artifact: Runnable code, tests where appropriate, and a short explanation.
mastery_checks: Can explain the concept without hand-waving.
Can identify common mistakes.
Can apply the concept in a small working program.

verification.expected_output: Learner can demonstrate the concept and explain it.

items.title: Deeper distributed systems
items.learning_objective: Understand and apply Deeper distributed systems in the context of professional Go software engineering.

zero_magic.problem_solved: This lesson explains what problem Deeper distributed systems solves for a Go developer.
zero_magic.why_it_exists: Deeper distributed systems exists because real software needs this concept to become understandable, reliable, or maintainable.
zero_magic.mental_model: Think of Deeper distributed systems as one step in the learner's path from raw code to professional engineering judgment.
zero_magic.under_the_hood: The lesson must explain the underlying mechanics rather than asking the learner to memorize behavior.
zero_magic.how_go_uses_it: The lesson shows how Go expresses Deeper distributed systems with idiomatic standard-library-first examples.
beginner_mistakes: Using Deeper distributed systems mechanically without understanding the reason.
Skipping error paths or edge cases.
Copying code without being able to explain the behavior.
zero_magic.real_world_usage: Professional Go code uses Deeper distributed systems in production code, tests, tools, services, or operational workflows.
zero_magic.proof_of_understanding: The learner must complete the practice task and explain the result in plain language.

path.notes: Electives are optional deepening tracks. They may reference the core path but must never block the core path.
